title: Kelita River
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Otheruses|River}}
The '''Kelita River''' was a [[river]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]].

==Description==
The Kelita River was a [[river]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]].<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref> Named simply for the [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']] word for moat, ''kelita'',<ref name="Mando'a">{{WebCite|url=http://www.karentraviss.com/page20/page26/index.html|text=Mando'a|author=Karen Traviss|work=[http://www.karentraviss.com Karen Traviss]|archivedate=20110925083955}}</ref> the Kelita River was located in Mandalore's northern hemisphere, forty-five degrees north of the planetary equator.<ref name="APM">''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]''</ref> The Kelita stretched for [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] through [[Tree/Legends|trees]] and over [[Grass/Legends|grassland]] in a winding path. In a single bend of the sprawling river, at the crest of a flat-topped [[Granite/Legends|granite]] hill<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref> the Kelita had carved away at over [[Time/Legends|time]], sat Mandalore's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]].<ref name="O66" /> Nearly surrounding Keldabe entirely, the Kelita formed something of a natural moat around the city,<ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref> a state which gave the river it's name.<ref name="Mando'a" />

==History==
At some point, a durable stone balustrade was erected as a barrier between the city's outer edge and the sheer drop from the hill top into the Kelita, whose fast-moving current was fed by several tributaries from across the [[Kelita valley]].<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref> A [[Mining sluice|sluice gate]] had once been erected for the Kelita, but over time it was removed and the narrow pathway leading to it from Keldabe was railed off. Patches of [[frond-grass]] grew in abundance in areas of Keldabe wet by the spray of the fast-moving Kelita River.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> A number of bridges were constructed to pass over the Kelita, allowing travelers to enter the city on [[Limb/Legends|foot]].<ref name="O66" />

As a young [[Youngling/Legends|child]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Venku Skirata]] found the rush of the Kelita River's current amusing, and during a trip into Keldabe with his grandfather and uncle, [[Kal Skirata|Kal]] and [[Fi Skirata]], the two paused to allow the [[Gender/Legends|boy]] to watch the river from the Keldabe balustrade.<ref name="O66" /> Several weeks later, [[Mij Gilamar]]—a friend of the [[Clan Skirata|Skirata family]]—had a run in with his former ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'' associate [[Dred Priest]] while visiting Keldabe. Upon learning that Priest had joined with the resurgent [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] splinter group, Gilamar [[Knife/Legends|stabbed]] and [[Death/Legends|killed]] Priest, and disposed of his body by throwing it into the Kelita River.<ref name="ImpCom501st" />

The Kelita River's woodland surroundings were severely altered during the [[Battle of Mandalore (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attack]] on Mandalore by the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. Large expanses of forested terrain around Keldabe were flattened, and portions of the surrounding area's land had been [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] by the Vong. However, the Kelita's fast-paced current was a boon to the damaged land, helping to wash away the toxins and purify the ground over time. For this reason, Mandalorians chose a [[Water/Legends|watery]] meadow near the Kelita as the site of a mass [[Burial/Legends|burial]] ground; mass graves were common in Mandalorian culture, as the Mandalorians believed they would all end up together in the collective [[Manda (religious)|afterlife]].<ref name="Sacrifice" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Kelita River first introduced to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe in the [[2006]] [[EBook|e-novella]], ''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' by [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]], becoming one of Mandalore's most prominently reoccurring natural features.<ref name="APM" /> The Kelita later appeared in Traviss' fourth and fifth installments to the [[Republic Commando|''Republic'' and ''Imperial Commando'' series]], entitled [[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel|''Order 66'']]<ref name="O66" /> and ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> She also included the Kelita in two of her entries in the multi-author novel series, ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'', namely [[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice|''Sacrifice'']]<ref name="Sacrifice" /> and [[Legacy of the Force: Revelation|''Revelation'']].<ref name="Revelation" />
